_sources.discrepancies: NCSL classifies CT as 'non-photo ID requested' (voters without ID may sign an affidavit). Connecticut enacted no-excuse absentee voting in 2023 via constitutional amendment. Early in-person voting was also added beginning with the 2024 election cycle. 2026 primary date (Aug 11) follows standard CT schedule; TODO verify for 2026 cycle. Registration deadline is 7 days before the election per CT law (online/mail); same-day registration available at polls.

earlyVoting.notes: Connecticut added early in-person voting beginning 2024. TODO verify exact 2026 primary early voting dates.
Early in-person voting at designated locations. Hours vary by municipality. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.
Connecticut added early in-person voting beginning 2024. No-excuse absentee (mail) voting also available. TODO verify exact 2026 dates.

acceptedIds: Any document showing name and address (utility bill, bank statement, etc.)
Non-photo ID is requested for first-time voters who registered by mail and did not provide ID at registration
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Connecticut does not have a statewide ban on phones in polling places. Voters should not interfere with others or photograph ballots. Electioneering is prohibited within 75 feet of a polling place.
additionalRules: No-excuse absentee voting enacted via constitutional amendment, effective 2023. Any registered voter may request a mail ballot.
Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. Unregistered or independent voters may not participate in partisan primaries.
Same-day voter registration available at polls with proof of identity and Connecticut residency.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request an absentee ballot without providing a reason (no-excuse absentee, enacted 2023)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Ballot request must be received by town clerk by 1 day before election; applications submitted in person by noon the day before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the town clerk by 8 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the town clerk by 8 p.m. on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.
